_meta.title: Pentax K-S2 WiFi HTTP API — annotated reference
_meta.description: Complete, hardware-verified map of the Pentax K-S2 built-in WiFi HTTP API. Every endpoint was probed against a physical PENTAX K-S2 (firmware 01.10). This file is both human-readable documentation and a machine-readable spec that drives the pyks2 library and its tests.
protocol_laws: LAW 1 (errCode-in-body): The HTTP status line is almost always 200. The REAL status is the JSON body's `errCode` field (200 ok, 400 bad request, 412 precondition failed). Clients MUST parse the body, not trust the HTTP code. Exception: genuinely unhandled methods (e.g. DELETE) return a raw HTML error page, not JSON.

LAW 2 (datetime formats vary): /v1/ping returns ISO-8601 (2026-07-15T11:43:15). /v1/photos/.../info returns YY:MM:DD:HH:MM:SS with colons (26:07:15:11:09:11). Numeric fields format inconsistently (xv seen as '0', '0.0', '-0.7'). Parsers must tolerate all forms.
LAW 3 (connection): Requests are most reliable with Connection: close and a fresh socket. Content-Type text/xml or x-www-form-urlencoded both accepted.
hardware_interlocks: AF/MF lever is physical, read-only over API, and fires NO change event.
Mode dial is physical, read-only over API (fires changed:camera).
Movie mode DISABLES the WiFi API entirely ('function unavailable in current mode').
Opening the SD card door KILLS the WiFi connection; camera drops to off/idle.
device params (ssid/channel/key) and lens params (focusMode) are read-only (PUT -> 400).
The camera's WiFi AP uses CLIENT ISOLATION: two client devices cannot talk to each other through it.
field_notes: exposureModeOption (in variables/camera) is empty ('') in every mode on the K-S2 (M/Av/Tv/P/autopict/TAv) — present in schema but unused on this body.

tvList emptiness signals tv-writability: non-empty in M/Tv/P/TAv (tv writable), empty in Av/auto/scene (tv camera-controlled, writes ignored). Same 'empty list = field not user-controllable in this mode' principle as avList being dynamic.
storages[].remain is a FRAME COUNT (shots remaining), not bytes/MB. Observed 6467 (near-empty), 3886, and 1 (one shot left).
battery is an integer percentage (observed 100, 66, 0) — not coarse buckets. status/device also has 'hot' (boolean thermal flag).

Lens identity is NOT exposed over WiFi: constants/lens is empty and both lenses report identical data. Only focusMode/focused/focusCenters exist. The only lens-dependent signal is the dynamic avList in variables/camera. You cannot identify the mounted lens via the API.
ISO AUTO is set via sv=auto (or AUTO); returns 200 and resolves to a computed value on readback. Numeric bounds validated (sv=999999 -> 400).
xv (EV comp) is writable across the full +5.0..-5.0 range and validated server-side (xv=+9.9 -> 400).

Per-mode value writability (via list emptiness) confirmed across the whole dial: P=av,tv,sv,xv | Sv=sv,xv | Tv=tv,sv,xv | Av=av,sv,xv | TAv=av,tv,xv | M=all | B(bulb)=av,sv | U1/U2=all | autopict/scene/AHDR=sv,xv.

Bare group roots merge facets: GET /v1/constants returns all camera mode-lists PLUS device identity (model/firmware/mac/serial) in one call; GET /v1/params returns camera settings PLUS lens focusMode PLUS device ssid/key/channel. Handy single-fetch handshake/state endpoints.
Bulb: shoot/start returns a shoot-style body (focused/focusCenters/captured:false); shoot/finish returns minimal {errCode:200}.

mode_dial.note: Full physical dial characterised over WiFi. Movie DISABLES WiFi.

positions.Sv: Sensitivity priority; sv,xv writable; svList has 28 finer steps
positions.B: BULB; av,sv writable; NO tv/xv. Capture via shoot/start+shoot/finish (200), NOT plain shoot (412)
positions.U1/U2: USER modes; behave like their saved exposure mode; all writable
positions.autopict: green Auto Picture; only sv,xv writable
positions.scene: SCN; 18 scene sub-modes selected on-camera; only sv,xv writable
positions.movie: DISABLES the WiFi API entirely (confirmed: ping fails)
positions.X: no separate X-sync dial position on the K-S2 (stays on B)
positions.gps: in exposureModeList but no dial position / no GPS on this body

endpoints.purpose: Self-describing list of all API endpoints.
endpoints.notes: Lists 40 endpoint templates. The authoritative surface.

endpoints.purpose: Cheap liveness check + ISO datetime. Ideal heartbeat.
endpoints.notes: No orange-LED / card-read risk. Use for connection health.

endpoints.purpose: Legacy flat superset of all state (K-1 style). Lists + values + device + storage in one blob.
endpoints.notes: Image Sync polls this relentlessly (618/691 requests in capture). pyks2 prefers the decomposed /v1/{group}/{facet} endpoints + the /v1/changes WebSocket instead.

endpoints.purpose: Static capability lists (the *List arrays): WBModeList, stillSizeList, shootModeList, effectList, filterList, exposureModeList, resoList, movieSizeList.
endpoints.notes: Fetch ONCE. These are the dropdown option sources for the GUI.

endpoints.purpose: Lens capability constants.
endpoints.notes: Empty ({errCode,errMsg} only) on the tested body/lens.

endpoints.purpose: Device identity: model, firmwareVersion, macAddress, serialNo, channelList.
Liveview capability constants.
endpoints.notes: Empty on tested body.

endpoints.purpose: Current camera settings: av, tv, sv, xv, WBMode, shootMode, exposureMode, stillSize, movieSize, effect, filter.
Write camera settings. Body: form-encoded key=value(&key=value).
endpoints.notes: STICKS and echoes the FULL camera state back (lists + values), so a PUT doubles as a GET. Validates server-side: illegal value -> errCode 400. IMPORTANT: avList is DYNAMIC (changes with lens/aperture state) — re-fetch after changes, never cache globally. Some tv tokens may be rejected depending on exposureMode. tv WRITABILITY RULE: tv is writable only when the mode reports a non-empty tvList (M/Tv/P/TAv). In Av and auto/scene modes tvList is empty and tv writes return 200 but are silently ignored (camera owns shutter). Check tvList emptiness before offering tv as editable.

endpoints.purpose: Current lens state: focusMode.
Attempt to write lens params.
endpoints.notes: REJECTED (errCode 400). focusMode is controlled by the physical AF/MF lever and cannot be set over WiFi.

endpoints.purpose: WiFi device params: ssid, key, channel.
Attempt to write device/WiFi params.
endpoints.notes: REJECTED (errCode 400). WiFi config is not settable over the API.

endpoints.purpose: Liveview params.
endpoints.notes: Empty on tested body.

endpoints.purpose: Read-only 'everything' view for a facet: params + lists + live values (battery, storage). facet in {camera,lens,liveview,device} or bare /v1/variables.
endpoints.notes: See variables-camera.json, variables-lens.json, variables-device.json, variables-liveview.json.

endpoints.purpose: Read-only transient runtime state: state (idle/capturing), focused, focusCenters, liveState, battery, storages.
endpoints.notes: See status-camera.json, status-lens.json, status-device.json, status-liveview.json.

endpoints.purpose: Per-facet slice of the legacy props blob.
endpoints.notes: props-device.json also captured.

endpoints.purpose: Fire the shutter (stills). Body: af=auto|on|off.
endpoints.notes: Returns {focused, focusCenters, captured}. `captured` is ALWAYS false synchronously — capture is async; detect completion via /v1/photos/latest/info or the changed:storage WebSocket event. af=off releases the shutter without AF hunting (correct for MF lenses / fixed-focus rigs). af=auto/on hunt for focus first. CARD-FULL: returns errCode 200 with focused:false, captured:false even when the card is full and NOTHING is written. A 200 does NOT prove a frame landed — always verify via latest/info path-change or the storage event. In Bulb mode plain shoot 412s (use shoot/start + shoot/finish).

endpoints.purpose: Bulb exposure START (open shutter). NOT movie.
endpoints.notes: CORRECTED: these are the BULB long-exposure mechanism, not movie record. In Bulb mode (dial=B) shoot/start returns 200 and opens the shutter; shoot/finish returns 200 and closes it. They 412 in non-bulb modes (which is why they looked 'unreachable' before — we hadn't tested with the dial on B). Plain /v1/camera/shoot still 412s in bulb mode; use start/finish instead.

endpoints.purpose: Bulb exposure FINISH (close shutter). NOT movie.
endpoints.notes: CORRECTED: bulb close. Returns 200 in Bulb mode after a shoot/start. See shoot/start.

endpoints.purpose: Drive autofocus / set AF point. Body: pos=x,y.
endpoints.notes: Returns focused:true on success. Works over WiFi even though focusMode (AF/MF) is read-only — you can trigger AF and set the point, you just can't switch AF<->MF.

endpoints.purpose: Enumerate photos on the card. Structure: {dirs:[{name, files:[...]}]}.
endpoints.notes: MYTH BUSTED: this does NOT hang. Earlier 'hangs indefinitely' reports were a keep-alive/bad-card artifact of the earliest probes. Verified reliable at 8 and 80 files, and even IMMEDIATELY after a 56s DNG download. Listing time scales ~linearly (~4ms/file). Safe to enumerate.

endpoints.purpose: Enumerate but cap the result to the first N files.
endpoints.notes: UNDOCUMENTED bonus: ?limit=N works, returns constant ~60ms/105B regardless of card size. Image Sync does NOT use it. Other params (dir, offset, page, count, from/to) are IGNORED (return full listing) — there is NO offset/cursor, so no true pagination, just a head limit.

endpoints.purpose: Per-image metadata (av, tv, sv, xv, orientation, datetime, cameraModel).
endpoints.notes: Works for ARBITRARY files, not just recent ones — enables full card browsing. ~252 bytes each.

endpoints.purpose: Metadata for the most recent shot. Special fast path.
endpoints.notes: captured:true once written. Poll this (or use changed:storage event) to detect capture completion. datetime here is the YY:MM:DD:... format (LAW 2).

endpoints.purpose: Download a JPEG preview (~54KB, 720x480-ish).
endpoints.notes: This is what Image Sync uses for gallery thumbnails. The ONLY working preview size.

endpoints.purpose: Small thumbnail (attempted).
endpoints.notes: Returns errCode 400. NOT supported on K-S2. Use size=view instead.

endpoints.purpose: Download the full raw DNG (~18MB).
endpoints.notes: ~55 seconds over WiFi. Same result as no size param. Too slow for bulk; fine for single grabs. For bulk ingest, use USB/SD card reader.

endpoints.purpose: Download the raw file (no size param = full DNG, ~18MB).
endpoints.notes: Identical to ?size=full.

endpoints.purpose: Delete a photo (attempted).
endpoints.notes: Returns a raw HTML '400 Bad Request' page (NOT JSON) — DELETE is not a handled method. Photos cannot be deleted over WiFi.

endpoints.purpose: MJPEG live view stream.
endpoints.notes: Content-Type: multipart/x-mixed-replace; boundary=--boundarydonotcross. Frames are 720x480 baseline JPEG, ~23KB each. Part-headers carry only 'Content-type: image/jpg' — NO embedded focus/exposure/histogram metadata (unlike K-1). Any overlays must be computed client-side or pulled from /v1/status. Point an at it and it just works in a browser.

endpoints.purpose: Digital LV zoom/pan endpoint — accepts POST but has no observable effect over WiFi on the K-S2.
endpoints.notes: CONFIRMED no observable effect over WiFi on the test rig: with an active liveview stream, every candidate param returns errCode 200 (zoom/level/scale/magnify/ratio/pos/x-y/on, incl. nonsense) but the live frame never changes. The endpoint is reachable and stream-gated (412 without a stream) and does not validate params. Cause unconfirmed — may require a hardware capability the test lenses lack (both are motorized-zoom kit lenses) or a camera state not reachable via the API. No usable digital zoom observed via the API on this setup.

endpoints.purpose: WebSocket event stream. Upgrades with HTTP 101.
endpoints.notes: Pushes {errCode,errMsg,changed:X} where X is a COARSE category. Only TWO values observed: 'camera' (any setting change: mode dial, aperture, ISO) and 'storage' (a file was written). NOT a value push — it's a 'go re-GET this group' poke. Fires NOTHING for AF/MF lever, half-press, SD door, flash. This REPLACES Image Sync's poll-storm: connect once, react to events, re-fetch only the changed group. Extended listen across varied actions produced ONLY 'camera' and 'storage' — the two-value vocabulary is exhaustive (no battery/idle/playback/lens events).
